---
title: "Project Home"
---

::: {.hero-panel}
<div class="tagline">Visual Analytics · Quarto + Shiny · AY2025/26</div>

# Singapore Tourism Recovery Prototype

## Core Question
How can one shared tourism time-series dataset support exploration, recovery-state clustering, and forecasting for Singapore's tourism recovery?

This site is an integrated workspace for:

1. Take-home Exercise 2 write-up.
2. Prototype module design and validation.
3. Team collaboration and onboarding.
4. Linked Shiny module development built around a three-module time-series app.

<div class="quick-links">
<a href="./take-home-ex2.html">Open Take-home 2</a>
<a href="./prototype/forecasting.html">Open Forecasting Prototype</a>
<a href="./app-guide.html">Open Shiny Guide</a>
</div>
:::

## Why This Structure

The information architecture is adapted from the strengths of these references:

1. [Habari Tanzania](https://thehabaritanzania.netlify.app/#team): one-page storytelling with section anchors and team visibility.
2. [Decoding Chaos](https://decoding-chaos.netlify.app/): Quarto-native multi-page prototype navigation.

## Workstreams

::: {.grid-cards}
::: {.card-block}
#### A. Submission-Ready Report
`take-home-ex2.qmd` is the individual submission page in Quarto HTML format.
:::
::: {.card-block}
#### B. Prototype Modules
`prototype/module-cluster.qmd` and `prototype/forecasting.qmd` define the clustering and forecasting module logic, parameters, UI, and output behavior.
:::
::: {.card-block}
#### C. Running Shiny App
`app/app.R` with modular server code in `app/R/`.
:::
::: {.card-block}
#### D. Shared Team Base
`team/onboarding.qmd` documents setup, roles, and branch workflow.
:::
:::

## Data & References Imported

1. `data/raw/Name your insight (4).xlsx`
2. `data/references/tourism_analysis_bilingual_plan.txt`
3. `data/references/tourism_data_cleaning_detailed_report.md`

## Start Development

```bash
quarto preview
```

```bash
Rscript run_app.R 3838
```
